repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
closes #30320
related #39830 

## Problem

Remote UIs do not have any proper background detection. For example, remote UIs
do not react to OS theme change.

## Context

A `--headless` Nvim server has no UI when it starts, so the OSC 11 background as
well as `'termguicolors'` (which only runs on startup `if tty`) checks are skipped.

Expectedly, attaching a terminal later with `nvim --remote-ui` does not re-run it. Servers will erroneously
keeps their dark default and ignore the terminal's actual theme/OS theme change.

Since `TermResponse` lacks a UI identity and `'background'` is global, nvim has
no way to deal with multiple UIs varying in, well, anything. ***This PR does not
solve any of that***. Instead, the most recently attached client wins in terms
of UI.

TL;DR The headless server simply never registers a `TermResponse` handler to act on
OSC 11 queries forwarded from the `--remote-ui` client. The most recently
attached client will determine the background.

## Solution

Re-detect on `UIEnter` when a `stdout_tty` UI attaches to a server that had no TTY
at startup.

These codepaths are (rightly so) an area of confusion, so here are a few subtle
facts I want to explicitly outline about the approach:

1. The attach path is registered only when no TTY was present at startup.
In contrast, a normal embedded TUI owns reactivity, so I chose
not to register `UIEnter` for it (would double-detect, emitting a spurious
second query). A headless server, conversely, could only ever learn the them
through this path. ***On each attach the most recently attached terminal wins***. [^1]

2. ***The server must actively re-query OSC 11 on attach rather than just listen.***
Although `nvim_ui_attach` and the forwarded `nvim_ui_term_event` both use the
same channel, I experimentally verified that because the client emits its query
"around" attach time, its reply can reach the server *before* `UIEnter` handler registers,
missing the client's initial one.

3. Explicitly set `'background'`s are never overridden (obeying precedence).

AI Disclosure: AI was used to write the tests.

[^1]: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/30320#issuecomment-4674312496

comment_data.body:
A `stdout_tty` attach now emits the OSC 11 bg query, so it precedes the stream payload. The alternative is setting `nottyfast` but this just seems more confusing...

Given the constraints I'm struggling to find a better solution here.

I wonder if we should ONLY do the UIEnter path?

What is the benefit of doing it on startup when there is no UI? I don’t think there is one. `'termguicolors'` and `'background'` are both UI options, they’re meaningless without a UI. So if we do this ONLY on UIEnter we might be able to simplify this.

-  if tty then
-    local group = vim.api.nvim_create_augroup('nvim.tty', {})

-    --- already set by the user.
-    ---
-    --- @param option string Option name
-    --- @param value any Option value
-    --- @param force boolean? Always set the value, even if already set
-    local function setoption(option, value, force)
-      if not force and vim.api.nvim_get_option_info2(option, {}).was_set then
-        -- Don't do anything if option is already set
-        return
-      end
+  -- Shared TTY option detection used by two paths: the startup path (a TTY is
+  -- present when Nvim starts) and the UIEnter path (a TTY attaches to a

comment_data.body:
See #37075 (forgot to link this in the original description)

The startup pass finishes background detection _before_ one's config & colorscheme load, so the colorscheme picks the right light/dark variant on the first paint instead of loading under the default and then flipping (exactly what #37075 fixed). 

UIEnter only fires _after_ config has run so detecting _solely_ there brings the flicker back (plus a startup OptionSet).

comment_data.body: I do see a better (and simpler?) option here: make the TUI block (not the server, as solidified in the mentioned pr) for receiving the OSC code _before_ `nvim_ui_attach`. If so, I can formulate this into a separate issue since this is definitely a larger(and separate0 discussion.

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
    -- The TUI client queries OSC 11 on connect, so that precedes the payload.
    local bg_request = '\027]11;?\007'
```

-    eq('Hello world', table.concat(read_data))

comment_data.body:
Why isn't this part of the above test? each "assert" doesnt need its own test.
It looks like the setup in the test is basically the same and this is just doing some extra asserts.

hmm, on closer inspection i guess they are arranging rather different circumstances.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4402,6 +4402,100 @@ describe('TUI bg color', function()
       eq({ true, 'light' }, { child_session:request('nvim_eval', '&background') })
     end)
   end)
+
+  -- Start a headless server (the outer Nvim is its OSC-11-answering terminal),
+  -- cleaned up when the test ends, and return its address and a client session.
+  local function start_headless(extra)
+    local server = new_pipename()
+    local argv = { nvim_prog, '--clean', '--headless', '--listen', server }
+    local job = fn.jobstart(vim.list_extend(argv, extra or {}))
+    finally(function()
+      pcall(fn.jobstop, job)
+    end)
+    local session
+    retry(nil, nil, function()
+      session = n.connect(server)
+    end)
+    return server, session
+  end
+
+  it('is detected on remote-ui attach to a headless server #30320', function()
+    command('highlight clear Normal')
+    command('set background=light') -- outer Nvim acts as a light terminal
+    local server, session =
+      start_headless({ '--cmd', 'colorscheme vim', '--cmd', 'set noswapfile' })
+    local screen = tt.setup_child_nvim({ '--remote-ui', '--server', server })
+    screen:expect({ any = '%[No Name%]' })
+    retry(nil, nil, function()
+      eq({ true, 'light' }, { session:request('nvim_eval', '&background') })
+    end)
+  end)
+
+  it('enables termguicolors on remote-ui attach to a headless server #30320', function()

comment_regular_data.body: @kylesower mind doing me a favor and taking a brief look at this pr? :thinking: no worries if not.

I think the approach is good. I played around with it a bit, and the only issue I found was that `nvim_ui_send` inside of `tty.request` will send the term request to all attached UIs; because of this, you get a race condition for what the bg will be set to if you're using two terminals with different bg colors. Admittedly, I don't think this is that important because you have to be doing all this remote UI stuff and use terminals with two different bg colors.

All this terminal detection stuff is a nightmare, though. I wish there were a simpler way. 

> make the TUI block (not the server, as solidified in the mentioned pr) for receiving the OSC code before `nvim_ui_attach`

This does seem like a viable option, which would also solve the above (very minor) problem if I understand your suggestion correctly.

BTW, the race condition is acknowledged in the original discussion linked issue - this is the exact downside of multiple UIs and for a later (maybe) refactor. The "race condition" (the "last attach wins") is sort of the design decision here.

Seems like we're in agreement?

Really appreciate you checking out the pr!

I don't think we're referring to the same race condition. This is what I did:

run nvim server in ghostty (light terminal)
run nvim client in ghostty (light terminal) -> bg set to light
run another nvim client in kitty (dark terminal) -> bg set to dark
run another nvim client in ghostty (light terminal) -> bg set to dark because all UIs get another OSC11 query, and kitty responds slower

But it's probably totally insignificant.

I think we're talking about the same thing. It's sort of like a reverse race... whoever is SLOWEST to respond (not necessarily last to attach) wins. ~~That behavior sounds expected (see the helpfile - "most recently attached")~~.

I've updated the helpfile and comments to be more clear about this

I'll wait for gregory to respond. I'll look into the behavior above later.

> I'll wait for gregory to respond. I'll look into the behavior above later.

I appreciate that you want my input but please don’t block on me, I am traveling a lot this summer and will have limited availability for reviewing things.

Sorry, I was unaware! Thanks for your prior comments. Adding Justin as a reviewer. Justin, I know you're working on like 7000 things at once - but I did see you resolve some flakiness related to these areas of the code and you also have context with the original issue.. Feel free to assign another reviewer.

Note that also due to #40720 there's a bit of a complex rebase here - please look over the implementation in this PR.
The diff is hard to read, but I know you and others are paying close attention to this area. So let's try it...

labels.description: Nvim defaults for options, colorscheme/theme, autocmds/events, etc.

title: fix(defaults): detect `'background'`/`'termguicolors'` on UI attach
